title: Tulak Hord

Additionally, [[Darth Zash]]'s dialogue in the [[Sith Inquisitor]] class mission "The Secret of Tulak Hord" in ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' establishes that Hord predated [[Marka Ragnos]], who reigned as [[Dark Lord of the Sith]] from at least as early as [[5100 BBY/Legends|5100 BBY]].</ref>

{{Quote|If you were to face an ancient Sith Lord in combat, you would learn that we are as children playing with toys compared to the prowess of the old masters.|[[Kreia]], referring to Hord's prowess in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]]|<ref name="KOTORII">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref>}}
'''Tulak Hord,''' known as the '''Lord of Hate''' and '''Master of the Gathering Darkness''', was a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] that ruled during the early [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]], commanding great armies and possessing a mastery of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]] which left him nearly unrivaled. During battle, Hord wore a black mask, making him a fear-inspiring sight.

==Biography==
===Lord of Hate===
{{Quote|On Yn, the Jedi had laid siege to an Imperial stronghold. Tulak Hord broke the siege single-handedly! And then he pressed forward to Jedi-held Chabosh, leaving nothing but Jedi blood in his wake. I never lacked Force to feed on in those days.|Khem Val|<ref name="TOR">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''</ref>}}
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Tulak Hord was a student of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and a pupil of the early [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]]. Rising through the ranks, Hord was feared by his rivals even in his early days, having mastered the art of [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|combat]] and manipulation of the Force.<ref name="TOR" /> During his early years, Hord fought an honorable duel against a [[Dashade/Legends|Dashade]] named [[Khem Val]], who later became his most faithful ally.<ref name="Khem Val">{{HolonetOld|biographies|khem-val|Khem Val}}</ref> Assembling a massive army of followers, Hord and Val led a campaign of terror against rebellious militants on the [[Planet/Legends|planets]] [[Yn]] and [[Chabosh]], crushing them. While on the campaign, Hord was approached by a Sith known as [[Aloysius Kallig]] who sought to serve him; however, Hord dismissed him. Kallig refused to accept dismissal, however, and challenged [[Unidentified general (Tulak Hord)|Hord's best general]] and beat him. After Kallig's victory, Hord decided to accept him, and he led Hord's troops to numerous victories. During the battles, Hord employed his knowledge of [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith rituals]], draining the strength from his opponents and feeding off of it. Earning the titles Lord of Hate, Master of the Gathering Darkness, and finally, [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], Lord Hord went undefeated, slaying all who dared to challenge his might. While Kallig's military prowess gained Hord enormous success, it also intimidated the Dark Lord, who feared that Kallig could become a very dangerous rival. Hord decided to deal with the situation by having Kallig [[Assassin/Legends|assassinated]], with his family subsequently going into hiding.<ref name="TOR" />

Lord Hord continued his endeavors into the arts of Sith sorcery, authoring many tomes of knowledge and crafting his personal [[Tulak Hord's holocron|holocron]]. Rumored to have known the secret to eternal life, Hord scattered much of his teachings in hidden and hard to reach locations to ensure only the worthy could obtain his knowledge. Discovering and conquering the [[Dromund system]], it was believed that the Sith Lord conquered over a hundred worlds for the Empire. One who did prove worthy during Hord's life was his only known [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], [[Ortan Cela]], who came to learn much from the great Sith.<ref name="TOR" />

[[File:Khem_Val.jpg|thumb|right|Tulak Hord's Dashade servant Khem Val]]
Appreciating his relationship with the honor-bound Khem Val, Lord Hord placed his servant and ally in suspended animation inside a [[Stasis chamber/Legends|stasis chamber]] deep in the heart of what would later serve as [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow's]] [[Tomb of Naga Sadow|tomb]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. Ortan Cela would kill his master not long afterwards by stabbing him through the back, and Cela's Dashade servant [[Veshikk Urk]] consumed the dying Sith Lord's Force energy.<ref name="TOR" />

At some point following his death, his loyal minions constructed a massive [[Tomb of Tulak Hord|tomb]] for their fallen master in the [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]] on Korriban. Entombed with him were several tomes of his writings, his holocron, and his [[Tulak Hord's mask|mask]].<ref name="TOR" />

===Legacy===
Following the conclusion of the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] and the bombing of Korriban by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], much of the histories of Lord Hord were destroyed or lost.<ref name="TOR" /> While the tomb remained intact, traps were in place to keep looters away and test the worth of any knowledge-seeking Sith. In [[3956 BBY]], during the [[Jedi Civil War]], [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] entered the tomb, successfully navigating its traps and discovering the Dark Lord's mask, taking it with him when he departed. Also within the tomb, Revan discovered several treasures, including three rare [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|lightsaber crystals]]: an [[Amethyst/Legends|amethyst]], a [[firkrann]] and a [[bondar]] crystal.<ref name="KOTOR">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref>

[[File:Hordstomb.jpg|thumb|left|The [[Tomb of Tulak Hord]], with a towering statue most likely modeled after the late Lord.]]
Before departing the world, Revan discovered [[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]] [[Jorak Uln]] hiding inside the tomb after the man was deposed by fellow Master [[Uthar Wynn]] as the Headmaster of the [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith Academy on Korriban]]. In the throes of his anguish and madness, Uln captured Revan and his companions, planning to torture them before executing them. While imprisoned, Revan and his companions joined forces with the captured Sith apprentice [[Mekel]] and defeated Master Uln.<ref name="KOTOR" /> [[3951 BBY|Five years later]], the exiled Jedi [[Meetra Surik]] investigated the tomb during her journeys, finding it to have been looted, and the entrance collapsed.<ref name="KOTORII" />

Following the return of the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]], some of Hord's artifacts were rediscovered. [[Darth Marr]], before gaining a seat on the [[Tenebrae|Sith Emperor's]] [[Dark Council]], discovered [[Tulak Hord's lightsaber|Hord's lightsaber]] hidden on [[Dromund Fels]].<ref name="Tulak">{{TORcite|type=codex|Legacy of Tulak Hord}}</ref> During the [[Galactic War]], Hord's [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] was one of several Sith spirits that manifested on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] for a brief period of time.<ref name="SoR">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Shadow of Revan]]''</ref>

During the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]], the [[Sith Inquisitor]] [[Sith acolyte/Legends|acolyte]] [[Darth Nox|Kallig]], a descendant of Hord's defeated rival Aloysius Kallig, awoke and rescued Khem Val.<ref name="SecretTH">{{TORcite|type=class|Sith Inquisitor|The Secret of Tulak Hord|Korriban}}</ref> Though initially hostile, the Dashade came to view the young Sith Lord as a worthy successor to both their own ancestor and his former master.<ref name="Dark Council">{{TORcite|type=class|Sith Inquisitor|The Dark Council|Korriban}}</ref>

==Powers and abilities==
Tulak Hord was renowned for his legendary dueling abilities. Centuries later upon inquiry from [[Meetra Surik]], [[Kreia]] remarked that Hord was "the greatest lightsaber duelist of the Sith Lords", going on to say that his skill was considered "remarkable even for his time, when many true lightsaber masters lived". Kreia also revealed that if Surik had to face an ancient Sith Lord in combat, she would learn that "we are as children playing with toys compared to the prowess of the old masters". Later, Khem Val revealed to [[Darth Nox]] that Hord ''single-handedly broke'' the sieges laid by Jedi to Imperial strongholds in the [[Yn]] and [[Chabosh]]. Over a thousand Jedi perished in these battles.<ref name="SWTORE">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]''</ref>

Hord was a fearsome master of the dark side. He was extraordinarily competent in the matters of [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinesis]]; when Darth Nox approached the ruins of the ''[[Endar Spire]]'', Khem Val revealed that "The great Tulak Hord once pulled a ship this size from the sky". Hord was also a master of sorcery.<ref name="SWTORE" />

==Equipment==
Hord wielded a [[Tulak Hord's lightsaber|lightsaber]] with an orange-colored blade.<ref name="TulakArt">{{ArtStation|username=chris_anderson|url=artwork/nOmxE|name=[[Chris Anderson (artist)|Chris Anderson]]|text=Tulak Hord}}</ref> He was very skilled in using it in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|combat]].<ref name="KOTORII" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:SWTOR_TulakHord_Heavy.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Tulak Hord Heavy Gear concept art]]

Tulak Hord's placement in the timeline was originally a point of some confusion. In ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', Kreia explains that Hord was a master of lightsaber combat and a Sith Lord of the original Sith Empire, but ''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' stated that the Sith Empire didn't have lightsabers. However, later sources, such as the ''[[Star Wars: Lost Tribe of the Sith]]'' series, established that the Sith Empire had lightsabers, and the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' and its associated materials greatly expanded on Hord, definitively placing him as a Dark Lord of the Sith prior to Marka Ragnos.
